selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly one hour daily on social media

messages.content: You must return JSON only. ALL fields in the schema must be present, even if null or empty.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":{"age":35,"gender":"woman","sexual_orientation":"asexual","occupation":"hairdresser","city_of_living":"Milan","employment_status":"self-employed","education_level":"primary school","parents_education":{"parent_1":"upper secondary","parent_2":"bachelor's degree"},"current_marital_status":"divorced","children":0,"migration_status":"native-born Italian","psychological_feature":"creative","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend roughly one hour daily on social media","hobbies":["videogames","volunteering","hiking"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":73,"level":"high"},"conscientiousness":{"score":87,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":38,"level":"moderate"},"agreeableness":{"score":1,"level":"low"},"neuroticism":{"score":69,"level":"high"}}},"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

persona = {"age":35,"gender":"woman","sexual_orientation":"asexual","occupation":"hairdresser","city_of_living":"Milan","employment_status":"self-employed","education_level":"primary school","parents_education":{"parent_1":"upper secondary","parent_2":"bachelor's degree"},"current_marital_status":"divorced","children":0,"migration_status":"native-born Italian","psychological_feature":"creative","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend roughly one hour daily on social media","hobbies":["videogames","volunteering","hiking"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":73,"level":"high"},"conscientiousness":{"score":87,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":38,"level":"moderate"},"agreeableness":{"score":1,"level":"low"},"neuroticism":{"score":69,"level":"high"}}}

response_raw: {"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":{"age":35,"gender":"woman","sexual_orientation":"asexual","occupation":"hairdresser","city_of_living":"Milan","employment_status":"self-employed","education_level":"primary school","parents_education":{"parent_1":"upper secondary","parent_2":"bachelor's degree"},"current_marital_status":"divorced","children":0,"migration_status":"native-born Italian","psychological_feature":"creative","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend roughly one hour daily on social media","hobbies":["videogames","volunteering","hiking"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":73,"level":"high"},"conscientiousness":{"score":87,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":38,"level":"moderate"},"agreeableness":{"score":1,"level":"low"},"neuroticism":{"score":69,"level":"high"}}},"opinion":"As a hairdresser in Milan, I see a lot of people coming in and sharing their lives, and social media is a big part of that. But with the good, comes the bad, and fake content is a real problem. I've seen clients get upset over things they've read online that just aren't true. It's important to know how to handle this stuff, especially since I spend about an hour a day on social media myself. First off, I always tell my clients to be skeptical. Not everything you read online is true, and it's important to question things. I've seen some wild stories shared on Facebook, and I always make sure to fact-check before I believe anything. There are great websites like Snopes and FactCheck.org that can help with that. Another thing I do is limit my exposure to fake content. I unfollow or mute accounts that consistently share misinformation. It's not worth the stress, and it keeps my feed cleaner. I also try to educate others when I see fake content being shared. I do this in a respectful way, though. I'm not the most agreeable person, but I know that being rude or aggressive won't change anyone's mind. Instead, I share facts and ask questions to make people think. I also try to be mindful of what I share. Just because something seems true doesn't mean it is, and I don't want to be part of the problem. I always make sure to verify information before I share it with my clients or on my own social media. Lastly, I take breaks from social media. It's easy to get caught up in the drama, but stepping back can help put things into perspective. I love hiking, and spending time in nature helps me clear my mind and remember what's really important. Fake content on social media is a big issue, but there are ways to manage it. By being skeptical, limiting exposure, educating others, being mindful of what I share, and taking breaks, I can navigate the online world more safely and responsibly.","reasoning_summary":"The persona is a 35-year-old hairdresser in Milan who spends about an hour daily on social media. Given her high conscientiousness and openness, she values accuracy and truth. Her low agreeableness suggests she might struggle with tact, but she can overcome this by focusing on facts and asking questions. Her high neuroticism indicates she might be more affected by fake content, making it important for her to limit exposure and take breaks. Her hobbies and lifestyle suggest she values authenticity and real-life connections, which can help her navigate online misinformation.","tone":"Direct, factual, and slightly assertive, with a focus on practical solutions.","citations":["Snopes","FactCheck.org"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["age","gender","occupation","city_of_living","education_level","time_onsocialmedia","hobbies","ocean","psychological_feature"]}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly one hour daily on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As a hairdresser in Milan, I see a lot of people coming in and sharing their lives, and social media is a big part of that. But with the good, comes the bad, and fake content is a real problem. I've seen clients get upset over things they've read online that just aren't true. It's important to know how to handle this stuff, especially since I spend about an hour a day on social media myself. First off, I always tell my clients to be skeptical. Not everything you read online is true, and it's important to question things. I've seen some wild stories shared on Facebook, and I always make sure to fact-check before I believe anything. There are great websites like Snopes and FactCheck.org that can help with that. Another thing I do is limit my exposure to fake content. I unfollow or mute accounts that consistently share misinformation. It's not worth the stress, and it keeps my feed cleaner. I also try to educate others when I see fake content being shared. I do this in a respectful way, though. I'm not the most agreeable person, but I know that being rude or aggressive won't change anyone's mind. Instead, I share facts and ask questions to make people think. I also try to be mindful of what I share. Just because something seems true doesn't mean it is, and I don't want to be part of the problem. I always make sure to verify information before I share it with my clients or on my own social media. Lastly, I take breaks from social media. It's easy to get caught up in the drama, but stepping back can help put things into perspective. I love hiking, and spending time in nature helps me clear my mind and remember what's really important. Fake content on social media is a big issue, but there are ways to manage it. By being skeptical, limiting exposure, educating others, being mindful of what I share, and taking breaks, I can navigate the online world more safely and responsibly.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: The persona is a 35-year-old hairdresser in Milan who spends about an hour daily on social media. Given her high conscientiousness and openness, she values accuracy and truth. Her low agreeableness suggests she might struggle with tact, but she can overcome this by focusing on facts and asking questions. Her high neuroticism indicates she might be more affected by fake content, making it important for her to limit exposure and take breaks. Her hobbies and lifestyle suggest she values authenticity and real-life connections, which can help her navigate online misinformation.
response_parsed.tone: Direct, factual, and slightly assertive, with a focus on practical solutions.
